content.problemStatement:
Transform the algebraic expression with brackets into RPN form. 



You can assume that for the test cases below only single letters will be used, brackets [] will not be used and each expression has only one RPN form (no expressions like a*b*c)



Input Format

The first line contains t, the number of test cases (less then 100).
Followed by t lines, containing an expression to be translated to RPN form, where the length of the expression is less then 400.


Output Format
The expressions in RPN form, one per line.
constraints: Expressions are fully parenthesized and contain only lowercase letters and the operators +, -, *, /, ^
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T < 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single fully‑parenthesized algebraic expression consisting of lowercase letters, the operators +, -, *, /, ^ and parentheses '(' and ')'. The length of each expression is less than 400 characters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing the expression converted to Reverse Polish Notation (postfix form).

sampleTestCases.explanation: The inner parentheses give b*c → bc*. Then a plus that result gives abc*+.
Compute a+b → ab+, compute z+x → zx+, then multiply the two results → ab+zx+*.
a+t → at+, b+(a+c) → bac++, c+d → cd+, exponentiation → bac++cd+^, finally multiplication with at+ → at+bac++cd+^*.

solution.approach: Traverse the expression from left to right. Whenever a lowercase letter (operand) is seen, append it directly to the output. Push '(' onto a stack. For an operator, push it onto the stack (the expression is fully parenthesized, so operator precedence does not matter). When a ')' is encountered, pop from the stack and append to output until the matching '(' is removed. After processing the whole string, pop any remaining operators. This yields the unique RPN form because the input is fully parenthesized and contains no ambiguous associative chains.
solution.timeComplexity: O(L) per test case, where L is the length of the expression. Overall O(total length of all expressions).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(L) auxiliary stack space per test case.
